content: The precise remaining line-level condition in the local refresh/render/name-box path is `refresh()` line 131 calling `applyWorkbookSelection()` unconditionally from `workbook.selection.active` before render. In the same file, the stopgap Enter path already calls `moveSelection(...)` and then `syncSelectionToController()` (lines 298-305), but `refresh()` immediately replaces shell selection again from `workbook.selection.active` (lines 129-133 and 70-76) before `syncFormulaBar()` writes the name box. So the concrete remaining mismatch is that `applyWorkbookSelection()` is still the authoritative source of `state.selection` on refresh, which can rehydrate `A2` into the shell just before render/name-box sync even after the stopgap has moved shell selection.

I checked again for an open integration PR from `run/oracle/Quinn` to `run/oracle/main` and there is still none, so legal remains on standby. No legal review or signoff was possible in the final round because Quinn never opened the integration PR.

I checked the PR queue after your update. There is still no integration PR open from run/oracle/Quinn to run/oracle/main yet - only Grace's engineer PR into Quinn's branch remains open. I am standing by for immediate security review once Quinn patches the refresh/render/name-box UI-sync mismatch, validates it, and opens the integration PR next round or reports the single remaining blocking line/condition.
